content: flutter_rust_bridge 2.x progress callback pattern: define a Rust function with a StreamSink<ProgressEvent> argument. FRB2 generates a Dart Stream<ProgressEvent>. On the Rust side, the function writes to the sink inside a thread (std::thread::spawn) so it does not block the async executor. The existing diffusion-rs API uses mpsc::Sender<Progress> internally — for FRB2, the GUI bridge crate wraps gen_img_with_progress, creates the mpsc channel internally, runs gen_img_with_progress in a spawned thread, and forwards Progress events through a StreamSink<ProgressEvent> that FRB2 provides. The Progress struct fields (step, steps, time) are private — the bridge crate must expose a new public ProgressEvent struct with the same fields.
